name: Warcraft instant simple workflow + beads-aligned manual tasks

description: Baseline setup attempt: added autoresearch harness and instant-workflow eval, but the benchmark crashed because eval/instant-workflow-score.ts imported warcraft-core via an unresolved package name.

Baseline: added autoresearch harness and a behavior-oriented instant-workflow eval. Current score is 1/5: status can continue manual tasks without a plan, but feature creation guidance, instant workflow promotion, self-contained manual specs, and prompt/system guidance are still missing.

Add an instant workflow for tiny tasks: manual task creation now accepts self-contained descriptions, auto-promotes no-plan features into workflowPath=instant/executing, manual specs fall back to the task brief, prompts/system/docs explain when to skip plans, and status can recommend the direct-task path.

name: Warcraft instant workflow + beads-native brief persistence

description: First v2 baseline attempt broadened the benchmark with a beads-mode persistence check, but the mock repository bypassed the real encode/decode path, so the 6/6 score was not trustworthy. Discarding this benchmark shape and fixing the workload before evaluating improvements.

Corrected the v2 benchmark to exercise the real beads encode/decode path, revealing a true 5/6 baseline. Discarding this run only to clear the contaminated prior v2 baseline; re-initialize the experiment next with the corrected workload.

name: Warcraft instant workflow + beads-native brief persistence (corrected workload)

description: Reset the v2 workload with a corrected beads-mode persistence check that exercises the real artifact encode/decode path. Trustworthy baseline is now 5/6: the remaining gap is that beads-backed manual tasks lose their self-contained brief during decode/readback.

Preserve manual instant-task briefs through the canonical beads path by adding `brief` to the task-state artifact schema and conversions. This fixes the real beads encode/decode gap, keeps manual specs self-contained in beads mode too, and expands the correctness checks to cover artifactSchemas round-trips.

name: Warcraft instant workflow + request-based path analysis

description: Broadened the benchmark again to include request-based path analysis, then implemented `analyzeWorkflowRequest` in warcraft-core and wired `warcraft_feature_create` to return `recommendedWorkflowPath` + rationale for tiny vs cross-cutting requests. Added focused tests and rebuilt warcraft-core so the new export is available through the package entrypoint.

name: Warcraft instant workflow + recommendation-aware status guidance

description: Broadened the workload again to cover recommendation-aware status guidance, then persisted feature-level workflowRecommendation from warcraft_feature_create and taught warcraft_status to suggest the right next action before any plan or task exists (notably the lightweight-plan path). Added focused tests and made autoresearch.sh rebuild warcraft-core so the benchmark exercises the real package entrypoint.

name: Warcraft instant workflow expansion fallback guidance

description: Broadened the workload to cover instant-workflow expansion fallback, then taught warcraft_status to stop blindly dispatching once a no-plan instant feature has multiple pending manual tasks. It now recommends switching to a lightweight reviewed plan instead. Added focused status/eval coverage and pruned the ideas backlog to the still-deferred full warcraft_task_expand helper.

Broadened the workload again to require creation-time expansion warnings, then taught warcraft_task_create to inspect no-plan manual-task buildup and warn when a feature has outgrown the tiny instant path. The tool now returns pendingManualTasks and nudges the user toward warcraft_plan_write with Workflow Path: lightweight, complementing the existing warcraft_status fallback.

Attempted to broaden the workload to add direct-task scope analysis, but the first pass overfit the structured instant brief format: analyzeWorkflowRequest treated Background/Impact/Safety/Verify/Rollback sentences as broad work, which broke the tiny-task path and the new eval. Discarding this pass and fixing the normalization before trying again.

Retried the direct-task scope-analysis workload with a normalized manual-brief input instead of naively feeding the full Background/Impact/Safety/Verify/Rollback block into analyzeWorkflowRequest. warcraft_task_create now records workflowRecommendation/rationale for non-tiny direct briefs, warns toward lightweight/standard planning immediately, and warcraft_status honors that recommendation before dispatching pending instant tasks.

name: Warcraft instant workflow escalation beyond lightweight limits

description: Broadened the workload to require escalation past the lightweight max-2-task limit and updated instant-workflow fallback accordingly. This is a real policy improvement, but the first pass regressed the 2-task outgrown guidance by letting a generic lightweight-recommendation message override the clearer multi-task fallback, so the score stayed flat at 12.

Broadened the workload to enforce escalation past the lightweight max-2-task limit, then made instant-workflow fallback internally consistent with that constraint. warcraft_task_create now upgrades manual-task buildup from lightweight to standard once more than two pending manual tasks accumulate, and warcraft_status preserves the clearer 2-task outgrown message while escalating 3+ tasks to the standard reviewed path.

Broadened the workload to require reviewed-plan scaffolds in task/status responses, but the first pass surfaced a real usability bug: the generated status scaffold pulled sluggy folder-derived task names instead of human plan titles. Discarding this iteration and fixing scaffold title sources before retrying.

Broadened the workload to require reviewed-plan scaffolds when instant/manual work needs to fall back to planning, then added `planScaffold` output to both warcraft_task_create and warcraft_status. The scaffold carries forward manual brief context into a draft lightweight/standard plan outline, and the retry fixed title sourcing to use human plan titles instead of sluggy folder names.

Broadened the workload to require ready-to-use plan-write handoff data, then taught warcraft_task_create and warcraft_status to return planWriteArgs alongside planScaffold. Agents can now promote outgrown instant/manual work into warcraft_plan_write directly without re-wrapping the scaffold by hand, while preserving the same reviewed lightweight/standard fallback behavior.
